items.title: Shannon entropy
items.description: H(P, Q) generalizes H(P): when P = Q, cross-entropy equals Shannon entropy. Entropy is the irreducible part inside H(P, Q) = H(P) + D_KL(P || Q).

items.title: KL divergence
items.description: D_KL(P || Q) = H(P, Q) − H(P). Minimizing cross-entropy on fixed P is equivalent to minimizing KL divergence to P.

items.title: Loss functions
items.description: Cross-entropy is the standard classification loss: empirical average of −log q(y) over training labels, implemented in frameworks as CrossEntropyLoss / sparse categorical cross-entropy.

items.title: Common distributions
items.description: Bernoulli and Categorical PMFs define P and Q in practice. Minimizing cross-entropy equals maximizing Bernoulli/Categorical log-likelihood under softmax or sigmoid parameterizations.
